---
title: crtools_Test          # Title of the document


execute:
  warning: false
  message: false

# Below will do html by default. To render the entire project
# Use the following code:
# quarto::quarto_render("HW2WriteUp.qmd")
format:
  html: 
    number-sections: TRUE
    code-fold: TRUE
    code-line-numbers: TRUE
    cap-location: top 
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    number-sections: TRUE
    reference-section-title: "References" 
    code-fold: TRUE
    code-line-numbers: TRUE
    cap-location: top 
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{amsmath}
          \usepackage[dvipsnames]{xcolor}
          \usepackage[shortlabels]{enumitem}
          \usepackage{parskip}
          \usepackage{hyperref}
          \usepackage{geometry}
          \usepackage{float}
    geometry:
      - top=0.75in
      - left=0.75in
      - bottom=0.75in
      - right=0.75in
format-links: [pdf]
---


# Loading File and showcasing ggmeansplot.R
```{r}
source("/Users/apple/Documents/GitHub/crtools/bootstrap_ct_model.R")
source("correlation_summary.R")
source("diag_ct.R")
source("fit_ct.R")
source("get_ct_family.R")
source("ggmeansplot.R")
source("interp_ct.R")
source("make_ggpairs_plot.R")
source("plot_ct.R")
source("refactor_data.R")
source("select_ct.R")
source("summary_ct.R")
source("summary_overall.R")


library(ggplot2)
library(ggeffects)
library(emmeans)
library(pscl)
 library(purrr)
data(mtcars)
set.seed(113)

# we can also use the count dataset
# data <- read.csv("../Dataset/McMillanAcheMonkeyTrips.csv")

# I used mtcars since and made artifical data instead
data <- mtcars
data$vs <- as.factor(data$vs)
data$am <- as.factor(mtcars$am)

# make a proper count outcome
data$y <- rpois(nrow(data), lambda = exp(scale(data$hp)))
data$exposure <- data$hp + 1
# add zero inflation
zero_idx <- sample(1:nrow(data), size = 5)
data$y[zero_idx] <- 0


#===========================
# cont x categorical poisson
#===========================



m1 <- fit_ct(y ~ hp * vs, data = data, family = "poisson")
ggemmeansplot(m1, pred = "hp", moderator = "vs")
emmeans(m1, ~ hp | vs, type = "response") # check if ggemeansplot give information on response scale
ggemmeansplot(m1, pred = "vs", moderator = "hp")

m2 <-  fit_ct(y ~ hp * vs, data = data, family = "negbin")
ggemmeansplot(m2, pred = "hp", moderator = "vs")



m3 <-  fit_ct(y ~ hp * vs, data = data, family = "qpoisson")
ggemmeansplot(m3, pred = "hp", moderator = "vs")


m4 <-  fit_ct(y ~ hp * vs, data = data, family = "zip")
ggemmeansplot(m4, pred = "hp", moderator = "vs")


m5 <-  fit_ct(y ~ hp * vs, data = data, family = "zinb")
ggemmeansplot(m5, pred = "hp", moderator = "vs")

ggemmeansplot(m5, pred = "vs", moderator = "hp")



# with offset one from each should be enough
m1_off <- fit_ct(
  y ~ hp * vs + offset(log(exposure)),
  data = data,
  family = "poisson"
)
ggemmeansplot(m1_off, pred = "hp", moderator = "vs")


m5_off <- fit_ct(
  y ~ hp * vs + offset(log(exposure)),
  data = data,
  family = "zinb"
)
ggemmeansplot(m5_off, pred = "hp", moderator = "vs")



# categorical x categorical

m6 <- fit_ct(y ~ vs * am, data = data, family = "poisson")
ggemmeansplot(m6, pred = "vs", moderator = "am")

m7 <- fit_ct(y ~ vs * am, data = data, family = "negbin")
ggemmeansplot(m7, pred = "vs", moderator = "am")

m8 <- fit_ct(y ~ vs * am, data = data, family = "qpoisson")
ggemmeansplot(m8, pred = "vs", moderator = "am")
  
  
m9 <-  fit_ct(y ~ vs * am, data = data, family = "zip")
ggemmeansplot(m9, pred = "vs", moderator = "am")

# I forgot the zinb case
m15 <- fit_ct(y ~ vs * am, data = data, family = "zinb")
ggemmeansplot(m15, pred = "vs", moderator = "am")



m6_off <- fit_ct(y ~ vs * am + offset(log(exposure)), data = data, family = "poisson")
ggemmeansplot(m6_off, pred = "vs", moderator = "am")



# cont vs cont

m10 <- fit_ct(y ~ hp * mpg, data = data, family = "poisson")
ggemmeansplot(m10, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")

m11 <- fit_ct(y ~ hp * mpg, data = data, family = "negbin")
ggemmeansplot(m11, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")

m12 <- fit_ct(y ~ hp * mpg, data = data, family = "qpoisson")
ggemmeansplot(m12, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")

m13 <- fit_ct(y ~ hp * mpg, data = data, family = "zip")
ggemmeansplot(m13, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")

m14 <- fit_ct(y ~ hp * mpg, data = data, family = "zinb")
ggemmeansplot(m14, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")



m10_off <- fit_ct(
  y ~ hp * mpg + offset(log(exposure)),
  data = data,
  family = "poisson"
)
ggemmeansplot(m10_off, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")

m14_off <- fit_ct(
  y ~ hp * mpg + offset(log(exposure)),
  data = data,
  family = "zinb"
)
ggemmeansplot(m14_off, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")

```


# Testing Emmean Text function

```{r}



mtcars$vs <- as.factor(mtcars$vs)
mtcars$am <- as.factor(mtcars$am)


em1 <- emmeans(m6, ~ vs | am,type="response")
mod.emmeans1 <- as.data.frame(em1)

em2 <- emmeans(m14_off, ~ vs | am)
mod.emmeans2 <- as.data.frame(em2)

cat(build_emmeans_text(
  model = m6,
  mod.emmeans = em1,
  pred = "vs",
  moderator = "am"
))

m10_off <- fit_ct(
  y ~ hp * mpg + offset(log(exposure)),
  data = data,
  family = "poisson"
)

em2 <- emmeans(m10_off, ~ hp | mpg)
mod.emmeans2 <- as.data.frame(em2)

cat(build_emmeans_text(
  model = m10_off,
  mod.emmeans = em2,
  pred = "hp",
  moderator = "mpg"
))


```
# Test Johnson Neyman Testing
```{r}

  jn$jn$conf.low <- exp(jn$jn$conf.low)
  jn$jn$conf.high <- exp(jn$jn$conf.high)

interactions::johnson_neyman(m1,pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")


original <- interactions::johnson_neyman(model = m11, pred = "hp", modx = "mpg")
new <- interactions::johnson_neyman(model = m11, pred = "hp", modx = "mpg")


cbands <- new$cbands


cbands$response_slope <- exp(cbands$slope)
cbands$response_lower <- exp(cbands$lower)
cbands$response_upper <- exp(cbands$upper)


p$x
 cbands

temp

jnplot(model = m11, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")


build_interaction_emtrends_tab(model = m11, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")


jnplot_res(model = m12, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")
jnplot(model = m12, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")


# still doesn't work for zero infaltion
jnplot(model = m13, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")
jnplot(model = m14, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")
m14$count

interactions::johnson_neyman(m13, pred = "predictor", modx = "moderator", type = "count")

str(m14)
interactions::johnson_neyman(m1,pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")
model$count
interactions::johnson_neyman(m14$count, pred = "hp", modx = "mpg")

str(m14)

unique(temp$Significance)


```


# Testing interp_ct

```{r}

W <-
interp_ct(fit_ct(y ~ log(mpg), data = data, family = "zinb"))
m14 <- fit_ct(y ~ hp * mpg, data = data, family = "zinb")

ggemmeansplot(m14, pred = "hp", moderator = "mpg")

t<-interp_ct(m6)
# cat + cat
m6 <- fit_ct(y ~ vs * am, data = data, family = "poisson")
ggemmeansplot(m6, pred = "vs", moderator = "am")

# cat cont
m1 <- fit_ct(y ~ drat * vs, data = data, family = "poisson")
t<-interp_ct(m1)

str(t)

m10 <- fit_ct(y ~ hp * mpg, data = data, family = "poisson")
t<-interp_ct(m10)


y <- rpois(1000,10)
x <- rnorm(1000,10,1)
x2 <- rnorm(1000,20,1)
z <- rbernoulli(1000, p = 0.3)
z <- as.factor(z)

data_temp<- tibble(x=x,y=y,z=z,x2=x2)

m <- fit_ct(y ~ x * x2, data = data_temp, family = "poisson")


 ggemmeansplot(m, pred = "x", moderator = "x2")
         

 
 em<-emmeans::emtrends(m1,specs =as.formula("~vs")  ,
          var = "drat"
        )
 
 
  em<-emmeans::emtrends(m1,specs ="vs"  ,
          var = "drat")

  
        em_df <- as_tibble(em)

        em_df[[paste0("drat",".trend")]] <- exp(em_df[[paste0("drat",".trend")]])
        em_df$asymp.LCL <- exp(em$asymp.LCL)
        em_df$asymp.UCL <- exp(em_df$asymp.UCL)
        
        class(em)
        
        
        # this turns the data into response scale
        em_df <- as.data.frame(em)
        
        # safe exponentiation (only meaningful for log-link models)
        em_df$trend     <- exp(em_df$trend)
        em_df$asymp.LCL <- exp(em_df$asymp.LCL)
        em_df$asymp.UCL <- exp(em_df$asymp.UCL)
        
        emmip(m1, vs ~ drat, type = "response")

        
        
        plot(em)
        
my_grid <- emmeans(m1, ~ vs|drat, type = "response", at = list(drat = seq(0, 10, by = 1)))

plot(my_grid)

        
        
```


```{r}



m <- fit_ct(
  y ~ hp * mpg + offset(log(exposure)),
  data = data,
  family = "poisson"
)

res <- build_emmeanscontrasts_tab(
  model = m,
  pred = "hp",
  moderator = "mpg"
)


txt <- build_emmeanscontrasts_text(
  model = m,
  mod.emmeanscontrast = res,
  pred = "hp",
  moderator = "mpg",
  alpha = 0.05
)



```
